student_profile: Incoming first-year university student starting September 2026, interested in quant finance, trading, technology, women-in-STEM and early-career programs.

programs.audience: Incoming first-year women, transgender, and/or gender-expansive students
programs.notes: Closest match for incoming first-year students before starting university.

programs.audience: Women, transgender, and/or gender-expansive university students
programs.notes: Multi-day program with separate tracks. Strong first-year target if eligible.

programs.audience: First-year undergraduates interested in STEM
programs.notes: Good after starting university.

programs.audience: University students interested in quantitative trading
programs.notes: Track from the master programs page because sessions vary.

programs.program: FOCUS / IN FOCUS / SEE / JSIP / Preview
programs.audience: Early undergraduate students; eligibility varies
programs.notes: These change by year and location, so the master page is the best source.

programs.audience: High school students in US/DC or Canada, age 13+
programs.notes: Very relevant before starting university if still eligible.

programs.audience: First- and second-year undergraduates
programs.region: New York, London, Hong Kong, Singapore, Sydney / varies
programs.notes: Invitation-style two-day program. Strong first-year target.

programs.audience: First- and second-year undergraduates
programs.region: New York, London, Hong Kong, Singapore, Sydney / varies
programs.notes: More market-making focused than Discover Citadel.

programs.audience: Sophomore women interested in trading
programs.notes: Likely not for incoming first-year immediately, but track for second year.

programs.audience: University students in software engineering, data science, STEM
programs.notes: University version of the Terminal competition.

programs.audience: Undergraduates interested in math, CS, data science, markets
programs.notes: Useful resume-building and can lead to interviews.

programs.audience: Students interested in trading; eligibility varies
programs.notes: More advanced but worth tracking.

programs.audience: Undergraduate students; eligibility varies
programs.notes: Track First Year Insights, Launch, Tech Labs, and Engineering Accelerator here.

programs.audience: University students
programs.notes: Good finance/trading exposure, not a recruiting program in the same way.

programs.program: Insight Days: Women in Tech & Trading
programs.audience: Women in STEM; eligibility varies by cycle
programs.notes: Strong Jane Street WiSE-adjacent program.

programs.audience: First- and second-year undergraduates interested in STEM/data science
programs.notes: Good early quant research exposure.

programs.program: Inside HRT / Explore HRT / Student Opportunities
programs.audience: First- and second-year STEM students; eligibility varies
programs.notes: Track HRT's student opportunities page.

programs.audience: Women / early undergraduate students; eligibility varies
programs.notes: Useful for quant trading exposure and networking.

programs.program: Women in Trading & Technology / Student Programs
programs.audience: Women in STEM; eligibility varies
programs.notes: May be later than first year, but track.

programs.audience: Undergraduate STEM students; eligibility varies
programs.notes: Strong quant firm. Mostly later than incoming first year but worth tracking.

programs.audience: University students interested in trading, software, and research
programs.notes: Track for internship and discovery-style opportunities.

programs.audience: Undergraduates interested in investment research
programs.stage: First year / early undergrad depending on graduation year
programs.notes: Not pure quant trading, but very useful for investing careers.

programs.audience: Students interested in options trading and quant finance
programs.notes: Track manually because exact event pages can move.

programs.program: Women in Quant Finance / Student Programs
programs.audience: Women in STEM / students interested in quant research
programs.notes: Good quant research exposure.

programs.audience: First-years and sophomores interested in investment management
programs.notes: Less quant-trading focused but strong early finance program.

programs.audience: Women and gender-expansive students in tech
programs.notes: Useful for newsletters, mentorship, and early opportunity discovery.

programs.program: Rotman International Trading Competition and similar competitions
programs.audience: University students
programs.notes: Good resume signal; usually team-based through university.

programs.program: Women in CS / Quant Finance / Data Science / Investment Clubs
programs.audience: First-year students
programs.notes: Often the fastest way to hear about invite-only events.
